title: Peruvian Shrimp Chupe De Camarones
description: A refreshing and light Peruvian dish featuring raw shrimp, sevaiiya, and ferment-jang, perfect for a summer lunch. This dish is a unique twist on traditional Chupe De Camarones, with a yeasty and bouncy texture.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the shrimp, sevaiiya, ferment-jang, lime juice, garlic, ginger, red pepper flakes, and salt, and mix well.
In a small bowl, proof the yeast by adding it to the water and stirring to dissolve.
Add the yeast mixture to the shrimp mixture and stir to combine.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, give the mixture a good stir and adjust the seasoning if necessary.
Serve the Chupe De Camarones chilled, garnished with additional lime wedges and sevaiiya if desired.
Prepare all the ingredients and tools needed before starting to cook.
Make sure to handle the raw shrimp safely and hygienically.
